findings.title: Account deletion / factory-reset fails to clean orphaned engagement notifications (wrong type names + wrong key column)
findings.rootCause: The factory-reset sweep at data.py:1430-1436 deletes engagement-notification orphans with `... AND type IN ('feed_liked','feed_commented','feed_reposted','feed_bookmarked')` and matches on `related_id IN (doomed_post_ids)`. BOTH are wrong: (1) those four type strings do not exist anywhere in the codebase — the real engagement notification types are 'share_liked'/'share_commented'/'share_reposted' (feed.py:122-129); (2) engagement notifications store the POST in the `post_id` column and the ACTOR's user_id in `related_id` (feed.py:133-135), so filtering by related_id can never match a post id. Net: the DELETE matches zero rows. The single-post delete (feed.py:806) and trip delete (trips.py:420) do it correctly with `DELETE FROM notifications WHERE post_id IN (...)`; the bulk reset path is the broken sibling — the very orphan class the migration f5a6b7c8d9e0 (NF3) added post_id to fix.

findings.title: Trip-invite notification is never cleared after Accept/Decline — bell badge stuck unread forever, re-click errors
findings.rootCause: handleNotificationClick deliberately SKIPS markNotificationRead for type==='trip_invite' (notifications.ts:287-290) with the comment 'the accept/decline routes already remove the notification server-side'. But respond_trip_invite (trips.py:962) never deletes the invitee's trip_invite notification — it only flips/deletes the trip_members row and notifies the INVITER. The modal handlers (share.ts:52-83) call respondTripInvite then close(), with no client-side mark-read or removal either (feed.ts:279-285 is a bare POST). So the notification is both un-read-marked on the client AND un-deleted on the server.

findings.title: Self-leaving a trip sends the leaver a notification saying they removed themselves
findings.rootCause: remove_trip_member supports self-leave (is_self_leave = actor == target, trips.py:1089) and only uses that flag to skip the planner permission gate. The trip_member_removed notification insert at trips.py:1148-1152 is unconditional and addressed to `target` — which equals the actor on self-leave — with message built from actor_name (the leaver's own name). There is no `if not is_self_leave:` guard around the notification.

findings.title: Notification titles/bodies for settled_up, settled_up_reverted and achievement_unlocked are hardcoded English for all locales
findings.rootCause: Notification titles render as `n.title || notificationDefaultTitle(n.type)` (notifications.ts:241). The server always inserts a non-null English title (settlements.py 'Settled up'/'Settlement reverted', achievements.py 'Achievement unlocked'), so notificationDefaultTitle's t() fallback never runs — and it has no case for these types anyway. localizeNotificationMessage (notifications.ts:122-154) has handlers for the common types but NONE for settled_up_reverted or achievement_unlocked, and its settled_up regex only matches the simple '{from} settled {amount} {currency} with you for {trip}.' form, not the recorder-variant body — so those bodies stay English. The i18n-drift test (tests/test_i18n_drift.py) only checks en.ts values against es/fr/pt; it cannot see server-side Python strings that never have t() keys.

findings.title: Following someone via the Friends page emits a 'Friend Request' notification whose body says 'started following you'
findings.rootCause: Friends.tsx:232 still posts to the legacy /api/friends/add, whose handler calls _follow(..., source='friend_request') (friends.py:207). _follow inserts a notification with type='friend_request' and title 'Friend Request' when source != 'followed_you' (friends.py:179-181), while the message is always 'started following you.'. The live /api/follows/<id> path (used by the profile FollowButton) correctly uses type='followed_you' + title 'New follower'; the Friends page uses the legacy façade, producing the mismatched 'Friend Request' label. The title is the server's literal string, so the frontend's t()-based title fallback never localizes it.

findings.title: settled_up / settled_up_reverted notification clicks dump the user on Home instead of the trip's Settlements
findings.rootCause: handleNotificationClick's switch has no case for 'settled_up' or 'settled_up_reverted', so both fall to `default: navigate(PAGES.HOME)` (notifications.ts:352-354). The function's own docstring (notifications.ts:261-263) states related_id is a trip_id for settled_up/settled_up_reverted, so the data needed to route to the trip's settlement page is present but unused.

findings.title: clearLocalState resets STATE.notifications but not STATE.notificationsTotalUnread (badge-count state diverges from documented full-reset invariant)
findings.rootCause: core.ts:118 sets `STATE.notifications = []` (and resets 8 other fields) but never resets notificationsTotalUnread. The parallel explicit-logout path in profile.ts DOES reset it (profile.ts:80), proving the omission is an oversight, not intent. The leak is largely masked because updateUserUI toggles the 'is-signed-out' body class (hiding the bell) when STATE.user is null, and the 401 path emits state:changed → updateUserUI; but the field still carries stale cross-user state that any future code reading it (or a render before the class applies) would surface.

findings.title: Unread notifications are never cleaned up — unbounded growth behind the LIMIT 50 window
findings.rootCause: The maintenance sweep deletes only `is_read = 1 AND created_at < -30 days` (main.py:786-789). Unread rows are intentionally preserved ('so the user's bell keeps surfacing them'), but with no age ceiling and a 50-row read cap, ancient unread notifications neither surface nor get reclaimed.
